components.title: SSH
components.description: Secure Shell (SSH) provides encrypted remote command-line access and administration for servers, network devices, and virtualisation hosts. Public-key authentication is enforced organisation-wide; the SSH daemon is centrally hardened via configuration management.
components.purpose: Provide encrypted, authenticated remote administrative access to organisational systems.

implemented-requirements.description: The SSH daemon is configured with Protocol 2 only — SSH version 1 is disabled organisation-wide via centrally managed configuration, removing the weaker legacy protocol's known cryptographic vulnerabilities.

The SSH daemon configuration (managed centrally via configuration management) sets ListenAddress to only the required management interface, a login banner, LoginGraceTime 60, HostbasedAuthentication no, IgnoreRhosts yes, PermitRootLogin no, PermitEmptyPasswords no, AllowTcpForwarding no, GatewayPorts no, and X11Forwarding no.

All SSH connections use public key-based authentication. Password authentication is disabled in the SSH daemon configuration (PasswordAuthentication no); only holders of an authorised, registered public key can connect.
SSH private keys are protected with a passphrase (key encryption key) before being issued to users. Unencrypted private keys are not permitted on any endpoint.
Where ssh-agent key caching is used, it is limited to workstations and servers with a screen lock, and the cached key is configured to expire after no more than four hours of inactivity.
